skill.name: brand-guidelines
skill.description: Applies Anthropic's official brand colors and typography to any sort of artifact that may benefit from having Anthropic's look-and-feel. Use it when brand colors or style guidelines, visual formatting, or company design standards apply.
skill.summary: Applies Anthropic's brand identity including official colors and typography to documents and presentations.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: All 21 static analysis findings are false positives. URLs in LICENSE.txt are standard Apache 2.0 license template references. Backtick patterns in SKILL.md are Markdown code formatting for hex color codes, not shell execution. No cryptographic code, network activity, or command execution exists. This is a benign brand styling documentation skill.

content.user_title: Apply Anthropic's Brand Guidelines to Your Documents
content.value_statement: Maintain consistent brand identity across all your documents and presentations. This skill applies Anthropic's official colors and typography automatically.
actual_capabilities: Apply Anthropic's official brand colors (dark, light, gray tones, and accent colors) to document elements
Style headings with Poppins font and body text with Lora font
Automatically fall back to Arial and Georgia if custom fonts are unavailable
Cycle through orange, blue, and green accent colors for visual elements
Use RGB color values for precise brand color matching via python-pptx
limitations: Requires python-pptx library for PowerPoint styling functionality
Custom fonts (Poppins, Lora) must be pre-installed for optimal results
Applies only to artifacts that support font and color customization
Does not automatically detect or style existing non-Anthropic branded content

use_cases.title: Corporate Presentation Styling
use_cases.description: Apply Anthropic's brand identity to internal and external presentations for consistent visual communication.

use_cases.title: Documentation Branding
use_cases.description: Ensure technical documents and reports reflect official Anthropic visual standards.

use_cases.title: Design System Implementation
use_cases.description: Quickly prototype and validate designs against Anthropic's brand guidelines.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Brand Color Application
prompt_templates.prompt: Apply Anthropic's brand colors to this document. Use the official color palette for all text and background elements.
prompt_templates.scenario: Quick styling of a simple document

prompt_templates.title: Typography Styling
prompt_templates.prompt: Format this content using Anthropic's typography guidelines. Apply Poppins to headings and Lora to body text with appropriate fallbacks.

prompt_templates.title: Presentation Slide Design
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a slide deck using Anthropic's brand guidelines. Apply official colors, fonts, and accent styling throughout.
prompt_templates.scenario: Full presentation creation with brand compliance

prompt_templates.title: Custom Element Styling
prompt_templates.prompt: Style all shapes and visual elements in this document using Anthropic's accent color rotation (orange, blue, green). Maintain brand consistency.
prompt_templates.scenario: Complex document with multiple visual elements

output_examples.input: A plain PowerPoint presentation about quarterly results
output_examples.output: Professionally styled slides with Anthropic's dark headings (#141413), light backgrounds (#faf9f5), and accent-colored charts using orange (#d97757), blue (#6a9bcc), and green (#788c5d)

output_examples.input: A text document with unformatted headings and body content
output_examples.output: Formatted document with Poppins headings at 24pt+, Lora body text, proper hierarchy, and brand-appropriate color contrast

best_practices: Install Poppins and Lora fonts on your system before applying typography styles for optimal results
Use the skill on new documents rather than heavily formatted existing content to avoid style conflicts
Review color contrast ratios when applying brand colors to ensure accessibility compliance
anti_patterns: Do not mix Anthropic brand colors with other corporate brand palettes in the same document
Avoid applying brand styling to documents that will be redistributed outside your organization
Do not rely solely on the skill for accessibility - manually verify contrast ratios for critical content

faq.question: What fonts does this skill use?
faq.answer: The skill applies Poppins font to headings (24pt and larger) and Lora font to body text. If these fonts are not installed, it automatically falls back to Arial for headings and Georgia for body text.

faq.question: Can I use this skill for non-Anthropic branding?
faq.answer: This skill is specifically designed for Anthropic's brand guidelines. For other brand identities, you would need to create a separate skill with different color and typography specifications.

faq.question: Does this skill work with file formats other than PowerPoint?
faq.answer: The core styling logic uses python-pptx for PowerPoint files. For other formats like Word documents or Google Slides, additional integration would be required.

faq.question: What are Anthropic's official brand colors?
faq.answer: Main colors: Dark (#141413), Light (#faf9f5), Mid Gray (#b0aea5), Light Gray (#e8e6dc). Accent colors: Orange (#d97757), Blue (#6a9bcc), Green (#788c5d).

faq.question: Do I need to install any dependencies to use this skill?
faq.answer: Yes, the skill requires the python-pptx library for PowerPoint styling. The fonts Poppins and Lora are recommended but not required due to automatic fallback support.

faq.question: Can this skill modify existing branded content from other companies?
faq.answer: Yes, but it will overwrite existing styles. It is best used on plain or unformatted content. For rebranding projects, review the results carefully to ensure all elements are properly styled.
